summary: I was born in the neighborhood of Austin in Chicago, Illinois, on July 14, 1897. Austin was my first home before my family moved to Oak Park. It’s where my early memories began, and it was part of the larger Chicago area where my parents settled after coming from Sweden.

Chicago was the city where I was born and where my family first settled when my parents came from Sweden. My father worked as a carpenter and later a contractor-builder in Illinois, and we lived in Chicago neighborhoods like Austin before moving to Oak Park. It was also in Chicago that my cousin called me about renewing the family farm lease in Sweden around 1915.

Illinois is where I grew up after my parents immigrated from Sweden. My parents were married in Elgin, Illinois, in 1893, and my father worked here as a carpenter and contractor-builder. We lived in places like Austin and Oak Park within Illinois, and it was the state where my family established their American roots.

Sweden is where my parents were born and where my family’s roots go back many generations. They came from the Uppsala area, north of Stockholm, and my ancestors were mostly farmers and tailors there. My father emigrated from Sweden in 1890, and my mother followed in 1893. We even considered renewing our family farm lease in Sweden around 1915 but decided against it.

My parents were born in the Uppsala area of Sweden, about forty miles north of Stockholm. Both sides of my family, including my grandfather and great-grandfather, lived in that region, working as tailors and farmers for generations. It was a place deeply connected to my heritage, even though we left it when my parents emigrated to America.

Stockholm is the capital city located about forty miles south of the Uppsala area where my parents were born. While my family was from the suburbs around Uppsala, Stockholm was the nearest major city in Sweden. It provides a geographic point of reference for understanding where my Swedish roots lie.

Pasadena, California, is where both my parents passed away; my father died there in 1931 at age seventy-one, and my mother in 1942 at age eighty-one. Although we lived in Illinois for much of my life, Pasadena became their final home. It marks the later chapter of their lives after their long journey from Sweden to America.

Elgin, Illinois, is where my parents were married in 1893 after coming from Sweden. It was an important place for our family’s beginnings in America, marking the start of their life together here. Though we later lived in Chicago neighborhoods, Elgin holds that early family milestone.
cues: start of family life in America

summary: After being born in Austin, Chicago, my family moved to Oak Park, Illinois, which became another home for us. I have early memories of living there, and it was part of the suburban area where my father continued his work as a contractor-builder. Oak Park represents the next stage of my childhood after Austin.
